(Egyptian Mythology)|lt=Ra|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=Ra|lt_zh=Ra|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1252904}}'' every morning. |- | Royal Coronation Festival Path|lt_zh=Solar Path|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ecliptic#Introduction|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q79852}} marking the beginning of a new cycle. |- | [[:en:Min festival|Min Festival]] | Royal | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Nehebkau Festival|de|Neheb-kau-Fest|ja|Nehebkau Festival|zh|Nehebkau Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1974477}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | [[:en:Beautiful Festival of the Valley|Valley Festival]] | [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]]: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re Festival|zh|Amun-Re Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q481558}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | [[:en:Opet Festival|Opet Festival]] | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | [[:en:Sed renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sokar Festival|de|Sokar-Fest|ja|Sokar Festival|zh|Sokar Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2298046}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Nehebkau Festival|de|Neheb-kau-Fest|ja|Nehebkau Festival|zh|Nehebkau Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1974477}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | [[:en:Beautiful Festival of the Valley|Valley Festival]] | [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]]: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re Festival|zh|Amun-Re Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q481558}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | [[:en:Opet Festival|Opet Festival]] | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | [[:en:Sed festival|Sed Festival]] | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | Death of the King | Burial by his designated successor and transition into the [[:en:Duat|Duat]] as ''son of [[:en:Osiris|Osiris]]''. |- |}
=== Cult of the Dead and Divine Festivals === In contrast, [[:en:Mortuary cult|Funerary Festivals]] did not fall within the category of royal festivals and fundamentally differed in their festive character. The same applies to ceremonies dedicated to deities that were not directly connected to the reigning king.

== Background == === Designation and Renewal === The priesthood designated as royal festivals only those celebrations exclusively reserved for the reigning ruler:
{| class="wikitable" style="width: 100%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! width="25%" | Festival Types ! width="67%" | Content Specifications |- | Divine Legitimation | After the coronation, used only as a written reference to the divine election, without a public celebration. |- | {{ill|Designation (law)|en|Designation (law)|WD=Q1200681|lt=King's Designation}} | The reigning king designates his successor. Rarely: designation by {{ill|Ennead|en|Ennead|WD=Q233458|lt=Divine Oracle}}. |- | {{ill|Coronation of the pharaoh|en|Coronation of the pharaoh|WD=Q2429635|lt=Enthronement Festival}} | The designated successor ascends the throne. |- | {{ill|Coronation (Pharaoh)|de|Krönung (Pharao)|ja|Coronation (Pharaoh)|zh|Coronation (Pharaoh)|lt=Coronation|lt_de=Krönung|lt_ja=Coronation|lt_zh=Coronation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coronation (Pharaoh)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1790650}}sfest | The throne holder receives the {{ill|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|en|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|WD=Q1413565|lt=Throne Name}} and the crown after the burial of his predecessor. |- | {{ill|Unification Festival|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|ja|Unification Festival|zh|Unification Festival|lt=Unification Festival|lt_de=Vereinigungsfest|lt_ja=Unification Festival|lt_zh=Unification Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unification Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2515140}} in the Coronation Festival | Symbolic unification of {{ill|Upper Egypt|en|Upper Egypt|WD=Q203751|lt=Upper-}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|en|Lower Egypt|WD=Q463871|lt=Lower Egypt}}. |- | Daily Renewal | The king ''appears together with {{ill|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|zh|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|lt=Ra|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=Ra|lt_zh=Ra|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1252904}}'' every morning. |- | Royal Coronation Festival | As an annual renewal following the completion of a {{ill|Ecliptic#Introduction|de|Ekliptik#Einführung|ja|Ecliptic#Introduction|zh|Ecliptic#Introduction|lt=Solar Path|lt_de=Sonnenbahn|lt_ja=Solar Path|lt_zh=Solar Path|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ecliptic#Introduction|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q79852}} marking the beginning of a new cycle. |- | {{ill|Min festival|en|Min festival|WD=Q1936130|lt=Min Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sokar Festival|de|Sokar-Fest|ja|Sokar Festival|zh|Sokar Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2298046}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Nehebkau Festival|de|Neheb-kau-Fest|ja|Nehebkau Festival|zh|Nehebkau Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1974477}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|WD=Q478205|lt=Valley Festival}} | {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re Festival|zh|Amun-Re Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q481558}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}''. |- |}
=== Cult of the Dead and Divine Festivals === In contrast, {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|WD=Q2445700|lt=Funerary Festivals}} did not fall within the category of royal festivals and fundamentally differed in their festive character. The same applies to ceremonies dedicated to deities that were not directly connected to the reigning king.

'''King's Festival Calendar''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} '''King's Festival Calendar''' (also '''Royal Festivals, Renewal Festivals''') comprised only {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|WD=Q115665045|lt=Procession}}s, which served to confirm and renew the reigning ruler.
== Background == === Designation and Renewal === The priesthood designated as royal festivals only those celebrations exclusively reserved for the reigning ruler:
{| class="wikitable" style="width: 100%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! width="25%" | Festival Types ! width="67%" | Content Specifications |- | Divine Legitimation | After the coronation, used only as a written reference to the divine election, without a public celebration. |- | {{ill|Designation (law)|en|Designation (law)|WD=Q1200681|lt=King's Designation}} | The reigning king designates his successor. Rarely: designation by {{ill|Ennead|en|Ennead|WD=Q233458|lt=Divine Oracle}}. |- | {{ill|Coronation of the pharaoh|en|Coronation of the pharaoh|WD=Q2429635|lt=Enthronement Festival}} | The designated successor ascends the throne. |- | {{ill|Coronation (Pharaoh)|de|Krönung (Pharao)|ja|Coronation (Pharaoh)|zh|Coronation (Pharaoh)|lt=Coronation|lt_de=Krönung|lt_ja=Coronation|lt_zh=Coronation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coronation (Pharaoh)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1790650}}sfest | The throne holder receives the {{ill|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|en|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|WD=Q1413565|lt=Throne Name}} and the crown after the burial of his predecessor. |- | {{ill|Unification Festival|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|ja|Unification Festival|zh|Unification Festival|lt=Unification Festival|lt_de=Vereinigungsfest|lt_ja=Unification Festival|lt_zh=Unification Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unification Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2515140}} in the Coronation Festival | Symbolic unification of {{ill|Upper Egypt|en|Upper Egypt|WD=Q203751|lt=Upper-}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|en|Lower Egypt|WD=Q463871|lt=Lower Egypt}}. |- | Daily Renewal | The king ''appears together with {{ill|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|zh|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|lt=Ra|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=Ra|lt_zh=Ra|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1252904}}'' every morning. |- | Royal Coronation Festival | As an annual renewal following the completion of a {{ill|Ecliptic#Introduction|de|Ekliptik#Einführung|ja|Ecliptic#Introduction|zh|Ecliptic#Introduction|lt=Solar Path|lt_de=Sonnenbahn|lt_ja=Solar Path|lt_zh=Solar Path|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ecliptic#Introduction|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q79852}} marking the beginning of a new cycle. |- | {{ill|Min festival|en|Min festival|WD=Q1936130|lt=Min Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sokar Festival|de|Sokar-Fest|ja|Sokar Festival|zh|Sokar Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2298046}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Nehebkau Festival|de|Neheb-kau-Fest|ja|Nehebkau Festival|zh|Nehebkau Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1974477}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|WD=Q478205|lt=Valley Festival}} | {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re Festival|zh|Amun-Re Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q481558}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Opet Festival|en|Opet Festival|WD=Q1194158|lt=Opet Festival}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | Death of the King | Burial by his designated successor and transition into the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat}} as ''son of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}''. |- |}
=== Cult of the Dead and Divine Festivals === In contrast, {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|WD=Q2445700|lt=Funerary Festivals}} did not fall within the category of royal festivals and fundamentally differed in their festive character. The same applies to ceremonies dedicated to deities that were not directly connected to the reigning king.

'''King's Festival Calendar''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} '''King's Festival Calendar''' (also '''Royal Festivals, Renewal Festivals''') comprised only {{ill|Procession|en|Procession|WD=Q115665045|lt=Procession}}s, which served to confirm and renew the reigning ruler.
== Background == === Designation and Renewal === The priesthood designated as royal festivals only those celebrations exclusively reserved for the reigning ruler:
{| class="wikitable" style="width: 100%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! width="25%" | Festival Types ! width="67%" | Content Specifications |- | Divine Legitimation | After the coronation, used only as a written reference to the divine election, without a public celebration. |- | {{ill|Designation (law)|en|Designation (law)|WD=Q1200681|lt=King's Designation}} | The reigning king designates his successor. Rarely: designation by {{ill|Ennead|en|Ennead|WD=Q233458|lt=Divine Oracle}}. |- | {{ill|Coronation of the pharaoh|en|Coronation of the pharaoh|WD=Q2429635|lt=Enthronement Festival}} | The designated successor ascends the throne. |- | {{ill|Coronation (Pharaoh)|de|Krönung (Pharao)|ja|Coronation (Pharaoh)|zh|Coronation (Pharaoh)|lt=Coronation|lt_de=Krönung|lt_ja=Coronation|lt_zh=Coronation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coronation (Pharaoh)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1790650}}sfest | The throne holder receives the {{ill|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|en|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|WD=Q1413565|lt=Throne Name}} and the crown after the burial of his predecessor. |- | {{ill|Unification Festival|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|ja|Unification Festival|zh|Unification Festival|lt=Unification Festival|lt_de=Vereinigungsfest|lt_ja=Unification Festival|lt_zh=Unification Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unification Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2515140}} in the Coronation Festival | Symbolic unification of {{ill|Upper Egypt|en|Upper Egypt|WD=Q203751|lt=Upper-}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|en|Lower Egypt|WD=Q463871|lt=Lower Egypt}}. |- | Daily Renewal | The king ''appears together with {{ill|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|zh|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|lt=Ra|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=Ra|lt_zh=Ra|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1252904}}'' every morning. |- | Royal Coronation Festival | As an annual renewal following the completion of a {{ill|Ecliptic#Introduction|de|Ekliptik#Einführung|ja|Ecliptic#Introduction|zh|Ecliptic#Introduction|lt=Solar Path|lt_de=Sonnenbahn|lt_ja=Solar Path|lt_zh=Solar Path|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ecliptic#Introduction|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q79852}} marking the beginning of a new cycle. |- | {{ill|Min festival|en|Min festival|WD=Q1936130|lt=Min Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sokar Festival|de|Sokar-Fest|ja|Sokar Festival|zh|Sokar Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2298046}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Nehebkau Festival|de|Neheb-kau-Fest|ja|Nehebkau Festival|zh|Nehebkau Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1974477}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|WD=Q478205|lt=Valley Festival}} | {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re Festival|zh|Amun-Re Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q481558}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Opet Festival|en|Opet Festival|WD=Q1194158|lt=Opet Festival}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|WD=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | Death of the King | Burial by his designated successor and transition into the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat}} as ''son of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|WD=Q46491|lt=Osiris}}''. |- |}
=== Cult of the Dead and Divine Festivals === In contrast, {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|WD=Q2445700|lt=Funerary Festivals}} did not fall within the category of royal festivals and fundamentally differed in their festive character. The same applies to ceremonies dedicated to deities that were not directly connected to the reigning king.
== See Also == * {{ill|Divine Shadow (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Gottesschatten (Ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|Divine Shadow (Egyptian Mythology)|zh|Divine Shadow (Egyptian Mythology)|lt=Divine Shadow|lt_de=Gottesschatten|lt_ja=Divine Shadow|lt_zh=Divine Shadow|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Shadow (Egyptian Mythology)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1539042}}

Vol. 33, 2 = ''Proceedings of the Egyptian Temple Conferences.'' Vol. 2). 4th Egyptian Temple Conference, Cologne, 10–12 October 1996. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1998, ISBN 3-447-04067-X. * {{ill|Erik Hornung|en|Erik Hornung|WD=Q117212|lt=Erik Hornung}}, Elisabeth Staehelin: ''New Studies on the Sed Festival'' (= ''Aegyptiaca Helvetica.'' Vol. 20). Schwabe, Basel 2006, ISBN 3-7965-2287-4. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|Siegfried Schott|zh|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q102228}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS).'' 1950, Vol. 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al., 1950.

{| class="wikitable" style="width: 100%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! width="25%" | Festival Types ! width="67%" | Content Specifications |- | Divine Legitimation | After the coronation, used only as a written reference to the divine election, without a public celebration. |- | {{ill|Designation (law)|en|Designation (law)|WD=Q1200681|lt=King's Designation|1=Designation (law)}} | The reigning king designates his successor. confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|WD=Q478205|lt=Valley Festival|1=Beautiful Festival of the Valley}} | {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re == Background == === Designation and Renewal === The priesthood designated as royal festivals only those celebrations exclusively reserved for the reigning ruler:
{| class="wikitable" style="width: 100%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! width="25%" | Festival Types ! width="67%" | Content Specifications |- | Divine Legitimation | After the coronation, used only as a written reference to the divine election, without a public celebration. |- | {{ill|Designation (law)|en|Designation (law)|qid=Q1200681|lt=King's Designation|1=Designation (law)}} | The reigning king designates his successor. Rarely: designation by {{ill|Ennead|en|Ennead|qid=Q233458|lt=Divine Oracle|1=Ennead}}. |- | {{ill|Coronation of the pharaoh|en|Coronation of the pharaoh|qid=Q2429635|lt=Enthronement Festival|1=Coronation of the pharaoh}} | The designated successor ascends the throne. |- | {{ill|Coronation (Pharaoh)|de|Krönung (Pharao)|ja|Coronation (Pharaoh)|zh|Coronation (Pharaoh)|lt=Coronation|lt_de=Krönung|lt_ja=Coronation|lt_zh=Coronation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coronation (Pharaoh)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1790650|1=Coronation (Pharaoh)}}sfest | The throne holder receives the {{ill|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|en|Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)|qid=Q1413565|lt=Throne Name|1=Prenomen (Ancient Egypt)}} and the crown after the burial of his predecessor. |- | {{ill|Unification Festival|de|Vereinigungsfest (Altes Ägypten)|ja|Unification Festival|zh|Unification Festival|lt=Unification Festival|lt_de=Vereinigungsfest|lt_ja=Unification Festival|lt_zh=Unification Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unification Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q2515140|1=Unification Festival (Ancient Egypt)}} in the Coronation Festival | Symbolic unification of {{ill|Upper Egypt|en|Upper Egypt|qid=Q203751|lt=Upper-|1=Upper Egypt}} and {{ill|Lower Egypt|en|Lower Egypt|qid=Q463871|lt=Lower Egypt|1=Lower Egypt}}. |- | Daily Renewal | The king ''appears together with {{ill|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|de|Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|ja|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|zh|Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|lt=Ra|lt_de=Re|lt_ja=Ra|lt_zh=Ra|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian Mythology)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1252904|1=Ra}}'' every morning. |- | Royal Coronation Festival | As an annual renewal following the completion of a {{ill|Ecliptic#Introduction|de|Ekliptik#Einführung|ja|Ecliptic#Introduction|zh|Ecliptic#Introduction|lt=Solar Path|lt_de=Sonnenbahn|lt_ja=Solar Path|lt_zh=Solar Path|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ecliptic#Introduction|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q79852|1=Ecliptic}} marking the beginning of a new cycle. |- | {{ill|Min festival|en|Min festival|qid=Q1936130|lt=Min Festival|1=Min festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sokar Festival|de|Sokar-Fest|ja|Sokar Festival|zh|Sokar Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokar Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q2298046|1=Sokar Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Nehebkau Festival|de|Neheb-kau-Fest|ja|Nehebkau Festival|zh|Nehebkau Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nehebkau Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q1974477|1=Nehebkau Festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival with New Year characteristics. |- | {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qid=Q478205|lt=Valley Festival|1=Beautiful Festival of the Valley}} | {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|de|Amun-Re-Fest|ja|Amun-Re Festival|zh|Amun-Re Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q481558|1=Amun-Re Festival}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Opet Festival|en|Opet Festival|qid=Q1194158|lt=Opet Festival|1=Opet Festival}} | New Kingdom: royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|qid=Q604644|lt=Sed Festival|1=Sed festival}} | Royal renewal and confirmation festival. |- | Death of the King | Burial by his designated successor and transition into the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}} as ''son of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}''. |- |}
=== Cult of the Dead and Divine Festivals === In contrast, {{ill|Mortuary cult|en|Mortuary cult|qid=Q2445700|lt=Funerary Festivals|1=Mortuary cult}} did not fall within the category of royal festivals and fundamentally differed in their festive character. The same applies to ceremonies dedicated to deities that were not directly connected to the reigning king.
